nits: Throttle UPDATE overwrites H.1's +5min tentative lease with +1min; a Lambda crash right after the throttle UPDATE exposes the row 4 minutes earlier than the H.1 contract would. Intentional per 'soft throttle' design.
Throttle path doesn't update last_polled_at; mild observability gap.
workspace_throttled activity attaches to latest open run for the workspace, may not be the run whose automation triggered the throttle. Per-spec misattribution.

H.5 is effectively dormant at default settings: H.3's POLL_PER_WORKSPACE_CAP=5 limits per-workspace rows in the SELECT to 5, well below H.5's cap of 30. H.5 fires only if operators raise H.3 above 30 or lower H.5 below 5. Defense-in-depth value is real but the guard is currently latent.
Counter increments before adapter call and is not decremented on failure — a workspace that throws on every poll still burns rate-limit budget. Bounded.
Test mock doesn't simulate H.3's SQL-level per-workspace cap; unit-test limitation deferred to H.7.
reviewer: opus-4.7 adversarial pass via Agent tool
